evidence.q1: 1. Definitions and Transaction Structure … 16. Governing Law and Closing Logistics; ‘Assumed Liabilities’ … expressly listed in Section 4; ‘Excluded Liabilities’ has the meaning stated in Section 5.
evidence.q2: ‘Assumed Liabilities’ means … Section 4; ‘Excluded Liabilities’ has the meaning stated in Section 5; Section 4 ‘Buyer assumes only the Assumed Liabilities’; Section 9 ‘Seller will indemnify … Excluded Liabilities’.
evidence.q3: ‘Within 60 days after closing’ and ‘35 days to review’ in Section 7 are precise durations.
evidence.q4: ‘This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.’ No archaic terms appear.
evidence.q5: ‘Seller’s indemnification cap is 15% of the purchase price, subject to a true deductible basket of $150,000 that applies only to losses above the deductible amount and does not recover from the first dollar.’ All sentences are grammatically whole.
evidence.q6: ‘At closing, Buyer will deposit 12% of the purchase price with the escrow agent…’ (Section 6) and ‘Seller retains all Excluded Liabilities, including all pre-closing taxes, employment, wage, benefit, discrimination, termination, and similar employee claims’ (Section 5) share a uniform modern legal register.
